_doc: Skeptic POV taxonomy. One file per POV camp to minimize merge conflicts.

nodes.label: Protecting Human Jobs Legally
nodes.description: This goal focuses on creating laws that keep human workers employed even as AI becomes more common. Think of it like a safety net that helps people retrain for new roles or keeps their current jobs secure from being replaced by software. The main idea is that society stays stable only when people have meaningful work, rather than letting companies automate everything just to save money.

nodes.label: Fixing Biased AI Systems
nodes.description: This goal is about finding and removing unfair patterns in AI that treat certain groups of people worse than others. It requires companies to test their software strictly to ensure it doesn't repeat historical prejudices. Just like a referee in a game, these rules ensure the technology plays fair and doesn't hurt marginalized communities.

nodes.label: Defending Your Digital Privacy
nodes.description: This goal aims to stop companies from constantly tracking you or using your personal data to build AI profiles. It pushes for laws that require your permission before your information is used and bans invasive tech like public facial recognition. It is like building a fence around your personal life so that powerful institutions cannot use your own data to control or judge you.

nodes.label: Proof of AI Bias
nodes.description: This data collects real-world examples where AI has made unfair decisions, such as in bank loans or court cases. It shows that when AI is trained on biased human history, it often repeats those same mistakes. This evidence proves that AI is not always neutral and can cause real harm to specific groups of people.

nodes.label: Tracking Job Losses
nodes.description: This data measures how many human jobs are being taken over by AI in offices and professional fields. It looks at tasks like writing reports or analyzing data to see how quickly companies are replacing people with software. This helps us understand the real impact of automation on the workforce today.

nodes.label: Ignoring Real Problems for Hype
nodes.description: This method points out that some companies talk about scary 'future' AI risks to distract us from the problems happening right now. It is like a magician using a flashy trick to make you look one way while they do something else behind your back. By focusing on science-fiction scenarios, they avoid taking responsibility for the unfairness and job losses occurring today.

nodes.label: Focusing on Real-World Harms
nodes.description: This approach argues that we should only make laws based on problems we can actually see and prove today. Instead of worrying about hypothetical future disasters, we should fix the bias, privacy, and job issues that are already hurting people. It keeps the conversation grounded in reality rather than chasing ghosts in the machine.

nodes.label: Mandatory Independent AI Testing
nodes.description: This method requires that AI systems be tested by outside experts before they are allowed to be used by the public. It is like having a car safety inspector check a vehicle before it hits the road to make sure the brakes work. This ensures that companies cannot just release dangerous or biased software without proving it is safe first.

nodes.label: The Risk of AI Echoes
nodes.description: This data tracks how AI-generated content is accidentally being used to train future AI models. It creates a feedback loop where the AI's own mistakes get baked into the system, making the data less reliable over time. It is like making a photocopy of a photocopy until the image becomes blurry and distorted.

nodes.label: Sharing AI's Wealth Fairly
nodes.description: This goal suggests that the technology used to run our world should be owned by everyone, not just a few wealthy companies. If AI can do most of the work, the benefits should be shared with society through programs like universal income. It is about turning powerful tools into a way to help everyone live better, rather than just increasing corporate profits.

nodes.label: AI Models Leak Private Data
nodes.description: AI models can accidentally reveal the private information they were trained on when users interact with them. Think of it like a sponge that absorbs water; if you squeeze the sponge, it leaks the water back out. This shows that models are not just tools, but storage containers that can potentially expose sensitive secrets.

nodes.label: Tech Workers Hold Real Power
nodes.description: The people who build and manage our digital world have the power to change how society works. Because they control the code and data that run everything, they can steer technology toward helping everyone instead of just a few wealthy owners. They are the new group that can reshape the economy from the inside.

nodes.label: AI Disinformation Is Overhyped
nodes.description: There is little proof that AI-generated fake news is actually destroying democracy or changing how people vote. Human-led campaigns are often much more effective at spreading lies than any computer program. We should focus on real problems rather than panicking over scary stories about AI that haven't actually happened yet.

nodes.label: Give Citizens Ownership Stakes
nodes.description: As AI does more of our work, we should give every person a direct share of the profits generated by these machines. Instead of just relying on a paycheck, people would earn money from the technology itself. This makes sure that when AI becomes more productive, everyone benefits, not just the companies that own the software.

nodes.label: Redefining Value Beyond Money
nodes.description: We should measure wealth by how much we contribute to our community and shared knowledge, rather than just how much money we make. By using digital tools to track these contributions, we can create a system where people are rewarded for helping others. This helps us move away from a system where only profit matters.

nodes.label: Sharing the Benefits of AI
nodes.description: We must prevent a small group of tech giants from controlling all the world's computing power. By making AI tools open for everyone to use, we can ensure that the wealth and power from these machines are shared by the public. This stops a tiny elite from becoming too powerful while everyone else gets left behind.

nodes.label: Jobs Shift Toward Oversight
nodes.description: AI isn't just replacing jobs; it is changing them so that humans act more like supervisors. Instead of doing the grunt work, people will spend their time checking, fixing, and guiding AI systems. This means we need to focus on training people to be managers of technology rather than just operators.

nodes.label: Fix Problems at the Source
nodes.description: Instead of trying to restrict how AI is built, we should focus on stopping bad outcomes when people actually use the software. Think of it like building a car: we don't need to limit how fast the engine can go, we just need to make sure the car has good brakes and seatbelts. This keeps technology useful while keeping people safe.

nodes.label: Building Systems That Recover
nodes.description: Complex systems will eventually have glitches, so we need to be ready to fix them quickly. Rather than trying to prevent every possible mistake before it happens, we should build institutions that can handle and recover from errors. It is better to have a good repair crew than to try and build a machine that can never break.

nodes.label: AI Is Just Another Tool
nodes.description: AI is a powerful invention, but it is not a magical or dangerous creature that will destroy the world. Like electricity or the printing press, it will slowly change society over time as we learn how to use it. We should treat it as a normal technology that we can improve and regulate through common sense.

nodes.label: Quality Over Quantity in Data
nodes.description: Building reliable AI requires carefully picking and documenting the information used to train it, rather than just scraping the entire internet. Think of it like cooking: if you use rotten ingredients, the final meal will be bad, no matter how much you cook it. By focusing on verified data, we can prevent AI from repeating harmful stereotypes and mistakes found in messy, unorganized web content.

nodes.label: The Hidden Costs of AI
nodes.description: Training massive AI models uses huge amounts of electricity and water, often harming the environment in poorer regions while tech companies keep the profits. It is like a factory that dumps its trash in a neighbor's yard to keep its own production costs low. We need to look at these real-world environmental impacts instead of just focusing on how fast the technology is growing.

nodes.label: AI Is Just Fancy Guessing
nodes.description: AI models are essentially giant math machines that predict the next word in a sentence based on patterns, not because they actually understand what they are saying. It is like a parrot that can perfectly mimic human speech without knowing the meaning behind the words. Because these systems do not truly understand the world, they often make things up or repeat biases found in their training data.

nodes.label: Losing Our Own Thinking Skills
nodes.description: Relying on AI to do our thinking for us can make our own brains lazier over time. When we blindly trust what a computer tells us, we stop questioning information and lose the ability to solve problems on our own. It is like using a calculator for basic math until you forget how to do simple addition without one.

nodes.label: AI-Generated Non-Consensual Images
nodes.description: AI tools are being used to create fake, explicit photos of people without their permission, which causes real-world trauma and harassment. This is a direct and dangerous misuse of technology that violates personal privacy and safety. It shows that AI can be weaponized to hurt individuals in very personal ways.

nodes.label: Sorting Real Risks From Guesses
nodes.description: We should focus on fixing problems we can actually measure rather than worrying about sci-fi scenarios that might never happen. Some risks are predictable and can be solved with better information, while others are just wild guesses about the future. It is like choosing to fix a leaky roof today instead of worrying about a meteor hitting your house tomorrow.

nodes.label: Sharing AI Wealth Globally
nodes.description: If AI takes over many jobs, we need a plan to share the money it generates with everyone, not just the people who own the tech companies. This would act like a global safety net to help people survive if their work becomes obsolete. It is a way to ensure that technological progress benefits all of humanity rather than just a few wealthy corporations.

nodes.label: Tracking Stolen Creative Work
nodes.description: Companies should be required to list exactly which books, art, and music they used to train their AI models. This creates transparency so that creators can see if their work was used without permission. It is like requiring a company to list all the ingredients on a food label so you know exactly what you are consuming.
